texto: animals are indeed more ancient, more complex and in many ways more sophisticated than us. They are more perfect because they remain within Nature's fearful symmetry just as Nature intended. They should be respected and revered, but perhaps none more so than the elephant, the world's most emotionally human land mammal.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Daphne Sheldrick sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. A afirmação “animals are indeed more ancient, more complex and in many ways more sophisticated than us. They a…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, animals, nature, útil em momentos de termino e reflexao.

frase_original: animals are indeed more ancient, more complex and in many ways more sophisticated than us. They are more perfect because they remain within Nature's fearful symmetry just as Nature intended. They should be respected and revered, but perhaps none more so than the elephant, the world's most emotionally human land mammal.
